slides.title: Numbers, Shapes, and Measurement
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 3 English, Unit 8: Numbers, Shapes, and Measurement. Welcome to Unit 8! In this unit you will use English to talk about maths in real life. You will count and name big numbers, all the way up to a million. You will learn the words for addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. You will measure things around you — their size, height, weight, and the distance between them — using words like meter and centimeter. You will also look for patterns and straight lines. Maths is everywhere: in the market, in the kitchen, and on the way to the masjid. By the end, you will be ready to solve a number puzzle, just like Isaac and Hannah do in this unit's story. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and say big numbers, up to a million
Use the maths words addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division
Measure the size, height, weight, and distance of things around you
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and say big numbers, up to a million. you will also use the maths words addition, subtraction, multiplication, and division. you will also measure the size, height, weight, and distance of things around you. Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: number, million, fact, pattern, straight, addition. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Maths Is Everywhere and A Pattern Poem. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Present Simple and Imperatives
bullets: The Present Simple tells about things that happen often or facts that are always true, like “The sun rises in the east.” An imperative is a command or...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Present Simple and Imperatives. The Present Simple tells about things that happen often or facts that are always true, like “The sun rises in the east.” An imperative is a command or instruction, like “Measure the height.” Both help us speak and write clearly, especially in maths. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1 — Measure and Tell. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
